metadata.seed: I have to repeat one of my classes over winter break, but I have plans with family. I will lose my scholarship if i dont pass

Sometimes it feels like I'm just spinning my wheels and not getting anywhere. I want to believe that it’s okay to struggle, but it’s just so overwhelming.

patient.query: I guess I just feel stuck in this loop where all I see are my shortcomings. I can't really think of the times I feel confident when I'm tangled up in that comparison. It's like I forget all the good stuff I've done when I’m worrying about what everyone else is achieving. It’s frustrating because I know I have my own strengths, but that voice just drowns them out sometimes.

I want to focus on the good stuff, but it feels so much easier to get caught up in the negatives. I feel almost paralyzed by it sometimes, like I can’t move forward.

patient.query: I guess I really do feel overwhelmed by it all, but it’s hard to put my finger on why certain things trigger that voice more than others. Sometimes it’s just a small comment or even just seeing someone else succeed that sets me off. It’s like a floodgate opens, and I can’t help but spiral. I wish I could just recognize it and move on, but it feels impossible in those moments.

I want to believe there’s a way to loosen that grip, but part of me is scared that it’s just how it is for me now. What if I can’t change it? It’s just exhausting to think about.
